meaning: vt.代替,取代,接替,紧接着...而到来,[律]延期vi.推迟行动
examples: Any one could supersede you as manager in my office.
In the future, robots will supersede human beings for very heavy work.
And Pharmacia's new injectable COX-2 inhibitor, called Dynastat, could supplement or even supersede morphine.
The report calls for a state-wide building code that would supersede local planning laws.
